title: ageratina collodes
intro: Ageratina collodes, also known as Mexican tea or Eupatorium, is a type of plant native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which have been shown to be effective in treating various health conditions. The plant's leaves and stems contain compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids, which are responsible for its health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system. Ageratina collodes can be consumed as a tea, made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water, or taken as a supplement in capsule or tincture form.
